1.1: Bram — keeps the Beaten Drum at the lower edge of Oakhollow; learned on arrival, as travellers do.

2.1: Thornwood — every deer-path, clean line, and sick fork of it; fenn's own country.
2.2: Thornwall — the old boundary wall on the north side, past which the corruption is spreading south.
2.3: Oakhollow — the town at the wood's southern edge, and the Beaten Drum at its lower end.
2.4: The old deer-craft lines — the true trails the deer themselves keep, older than the marked paths and clean of the rot.

content._: What Fenn has come to know — the names and terms recognisable. The soft reads this to decide who can be named; absence means perceived by appearance, and a learned name is appended here.
